_comment: 2026 federal tax parameters, single filer (verified against IRS Rev. Proc. / Tax Foundation, June 2026). All thresholds in nominal base_year dollars; the engine scales them by each path's cumulative simulated inflation. EXCEPTION: the Social Security provisional-income thresholds are not indexed in statute and are left unscaled (see _ss_comment). Refresh annually.
_ss_comment: Social Security taxability, single filer (IRS Pub. 915). ss_taxable_fraction is the statutory MAXIMUM share of benefits that can be taxed. The provisional-income thresholds are fixed in statute and NOT inflation-indexed, so the engine compares nominal income against them WITHOUT scaling by simulated inflation — intentionally modeling the SS 'tax torpedo' worsening over a long, inflating retirement.
